skill.name: literature-close-read
skill.description: Produce a structured close-reading report from a paper's full PDF-to-Markdown text (with `## Page XX` pagination and image references) when you need to systematically extract background, research questions, methods, results, limitations, and reproducible experimental details.
skill.summary: Systematically extract background, methods, results, and limitations from academic papers using structured close-reading reports.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 37 static analysis findings were evaluated and determined to be false positives. The 11 high-severity 'weak cryptographic algorithm' flags matched only Markdown section headers and JSON keys with no cryptographic operations. The 24 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' flags are standard Markdown inline code formatting, not shell commands. The 'system reconnaissance' and 'hardcoded URL' flags point to legitimate audit documentation and a GitHub source attribution link. No prompt injection, executable code, network exfiltration, or malicious patterns were found. This skill contains only documentation and templates for academic paper analysis.

low_findings.title: System reconnaissance in legacy audit JSON (false positive)
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged terms in the audit result JSON as system reconnaissance. These are standard audit fields evaluating scientific integrity and output scope boundaries. No actual system reconnaissance or information gathering behavior exists.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL in SKILL.md (false positive)
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged the GitHub source URL in the SKILL.md frontmatter. This is a legitimate source attribution link to the skill's repository, not a data exfiltration endpoint.

dangerous_patterns.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm (false positive - no cryptography present)
dangerous_patterns.description: Scanner flagged 11 locations for weak cryptographic algorithms. Manual review confirms zero cryptographic operations exist in any file. The flags matched Markdown section headers (e.g., '## Methodology and Experimental Design'), YAML frontmatter keys, and JSON scoring fields - none of which involve cryptographic algorithms.

dangerous_patterns.title: Ruby/shell backtick execution (false positive - Markdown formatting)
dangerous_patterns.description: Scanner flagged 24 instances of backtick usage as Ruby shell execution. All backticks are standard Markdown formatting: inline code spans (e.g., `pdf-extract`, `## Page XX`, `outputs/`) and fenced code blocks for example bash commands. No Ruby code or actual shell execution exists in any file.

content.user_title: Analyze Academic Papers with Structured Close Reading
content.value_statement: Researchers spend too much time manually extracting key details from academic papers. This skill automates structured close-reading reports, systematically capturing background, methods, results, limitations, and replication details.
actual_capabilities: Extract research background and problem statements from full paper text
Identify methodological details and experimental design elements
Capture key results and statistical evidence as stated in the paper
Document limitations and threats to validity mentioned by the authors
List reproducible points and follow-up research questions from the text
Generate structured Markdown reports following a predefined template
limitations: Cannot read PDF files directly; requires pre-converted Markdown text input
Does not infer missing information; only reports what is explicitly stated in the text
Cannot access external databases or verify claims against other sources
Output quality depends on completeness and clarity of the source Markdown text

use_cases.title: Graduate Student Literature Review
use_cases.description: A graduate student needs to systematically analyze ten papers for their thesis literature review chapter, extracting methodology and results from each.

use_cases.title: Peer Review Preparation
use_cases.description: A researcher preparing a peer review needs to extract methodological details and evaluate potential flaws from a submitted paper.

use_cases.title: Replication Study Planning
use_cases.description: A research team planning a replication study needs to extract all experimental parameters, datasets, and statistical methods from the original paper.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Close Reading
prompt_templates.prompt: I have an academic paper converted from PDF to Markdown. Please produce a structured close-reading report covering the research background, research questions, methodology, key results, limitations, and points for replication. Use the template provided in the skill's asset file. Do not speculate beyond what the text states.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user who wants a standard close-reading report for one paper

prompt_templates.title: Methods-Focused Extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: I need a detailed methodological extraction from this paper. Focus on experimental design, datasets used, training settings, hyperparameters, evaluation metrics, baseline comparisons, and statistical tests. For any missing methodological detail, write 'Not specified'.
prompt_templates.scenario: Researcher evaluating reproducibility of a specific study

prompt_templates.title: Limitations and Critical Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Read this paper and produce a close-reading report with emphasis on limitations, threats to validity, and unstated assumptions. Compare the claims made in the paper against the evidence provided. Flag any gaps between the conclusions and the data presented.
prompt_templates.scenario: Peer reviewer assessing paper weaknesses before writing a review

prompt_templates.title: Comparative Multi-Paper Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: I have three papers on the same research topic converted to Markdown. For each paper, extract the research question, methodology, key results, and limitations. Then produce a comparative table showing how the papers differ in their approaches, datasets, and findings.
prompt_templates.scenario: Researcher conducting a systematic literature review across multiple papers

output_examples.input: Full Markdown text of a paper about transformer architectures for medical image segmentation
output_examples.output: A structured close-reading report with sections for Basic Information (title, authors, journal), Research Questions and Hypotheses, Methodology and Experimental Design, Key Results and Data, Statistical Methods, Conclusions and Limitations, Key Points for Replication, and Future Directions.

output_examples.input: Markdown text of a clinical trial paper with pagination markers like '## Page 3' and image references like '![fig1](...)'
output_examples.output: A close-reading report that navigates the paper using page markers, extracts patient cohort sizes, treatment protocols, primary and secondary endpoints, statistical significance values, and flags limitations such as small sample size or short follow-up period.

output_examples.input: Markdown text of a machine learning paper describing a new neural network architecture
output_examples.output: A report capturing the model architecture details (layer types, activation functions, parameter counts), training configuration (optimizer, learning rate, batch size, epochs), dataset splits, baseline comparisons with numerical results, ablation study findings, and reproducibility checklist.

best_practices: Convert PDFs to Markdown using a reliable tool before using this skill to preserve all text and layout.
Review the generated report against the original paper to verify accuracy, especially for numerical data and statistical claims.
Specify your target output language in the request if you need the report in a language other than Chinese.
anti_patterns: Do not ask the skill to read PDF files directly; it only works with pre-converted Markdown text input.
Do not request speculative interpretations or conclusions that go beyond what the paper text explicitly states.
Do not use this skill for short texts or abstracts; it is designed for full-length academic paper analysis.

faq.question: Can this skill read PDF files directly?
faq.answer: No. The skill requires the paper to be pre-converted from PDF to Markdown format. Use a PDF-to-Markdown converter tool first.

faq.question: What format does the output use?
faq.answer: The output is a structured Markdown document following the template defined in assets/deep_reading_template.md.

faq.question: Does the skill add its own interpretation or speculation?
faq.answer: No. The skill strictly reports only what is explicitly present in the source text. Missing information is marked as 'Not specified'.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for non-academic documents?
faq.answer: The skill is designed for academic papers. It may work with other structured documents but results may vary.

faq.question: How many papers can I analyze at once?
faq.answer: The skill processes one paper at a time. For multi-paper analysis, make separate requests or use the comparative analysis prompt template.

faq.question: Does the skill support all research fields?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill is field-agnostic. It works with any academic paper as long as the full text is available in Markdown format.
